description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a distinctive camouflage pattern of blue, tan, and green polygons on its upper and lower wings, accented by bold black and white crosses. The fuselage is mostly black with white stripes and a cross insignia on the tail, while the nose is olive green and features a wooden propeller. The biplane has two sets of parallel wings supported by struts, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The overall design and markings suggest a World War I-era German fighter aircraft.
description: A vintage biplane with blue-tan camouflage wings, black-and-white cross insignia, a wooden propeller, and an open cockpit. The fuselage is black with white stripes and the landing gear is fixed.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan with blue-tan camouflage and cross insignia, as well as the black and white striped tail.
description_view_1: Angled side-rear view highlighting the tail section, cross insignia, and the fixed landing gear beneath the fuselage.
description_view_2: Frontal view displaying the wooden propeller, olive green nose, open cockpit, and the biplane's dual wing structure.
description_view_3: Angled side-front view showing the propeller, nose, cockpit, and the distinctive camouflage pattern on the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
